commit.message:
feat: embed Solana Organic Hunter methodology into system prompt

Add a structured workflow to the Gemini system instruction for
identifying tokens with genuine organic community engagement.
Includes core criteria (tx density, unique wallets, vol/liq ratio,
narrative), a 5-step discovery-to-reporting workflow, Organic Score
(1-10) format, and hard safety guardrails.

No new dependencies or MCP servers — uses existing DexScreener,
svm402, RugCheck, and Solana RPC tools.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci(release): publish GitHub releases with DMG + EXE only

Add a dedicated release workflow that builds and publishes exactly two assets
for each tagged release:
- AlarmViewer.exe (Windows)
- AlarmViewer-macOS.dmg (macOS)

Workflow behavior:
- builds Windows EXE and macOS DMG in parallel
- waits for both builds to succeed before publishing
- generates release notes with full commit and changed-file lists
- uploads only EXE + DMG to the GitHub release

Also remove automatic release-publish hooks from legacy platform workflows so
installer artifacts are no longer auto-attached on release events.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix mobile player: touch seeking, configurable time color, smaller buttons, collapsible volume (#51)

* Fix mobile player: touch seeking, configurable time color, smaller buttons, collapsible volume

- Add onTouchStart/onTouchMove/onTouchEnd handlers to the waveform canvas so
  users can tap and scrub to seek on touch devices; preventDefault stops
  page-scroll interference during a drag
- Add timeColor to WaveformNavigatorStyles and WaveformControlsStyles so callers
  can control the playback-time text colour; applies via inline style instead of
  the previously hardcoded #374151
- Reduce mobile button sizes (play 48→44px, rewind/forward 38→34px) and tighten
  the center gap for a less crowded layout on small screens
- Collapse the volume slider on mobile (≤480px) by default; tapping the speaker
  icon toggles visibility and expands the slider into a full-width row below the
  controls; on desktop the existing mute/unmute behaviour is preserved

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JMkf4XFMoJmBvVEJjErkXN

* Add unit and E2E tests for mobile player behaviour

Unit tests (src/__tests__/WaveformNavigator.mobile.test.tsx):
- Touch seeking: touchStart seeks to correct position, clamps at edges,
  touchMove scrubs continuously, tooltip appears on touch and hides on touchEnd
- timeColor prop: custom colour applied as inline style, default colour used
  when prop is absent, distinct colours render distinctly
- Collapsible volume: speaker click adds/removes volume-open class on mobile
  (via window.matchMedia mock), desktop clicks do not add volume-open class,
  volume slider input is always present in the DOM

Playwright E2E tests (e2e/mobile.spec.ts):
- Mobile layout: canvas fits within 375 px viewport, play/rewind/forward
  buttons and time display are all visible, play button is smaller on mobile
  than on desktop
- Touch seeking: tap at 75 % of canvas width changes displayed time, drag
  across canvas updates position without errors
- Collapsible volume: slider hidden by default at mobile breakpoint, speaker
  tap shows it, second tap hides it again, volume-open class reflects state,
  slider is always visible at desktop width

Added mobile-chrome Playwright project (Pixel 5 device) scoped to
mobile.spec.ts so touch-event emulation is available in CI.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JMkf4XFMoJmBvVEJjErkXN

* Fix curly and Prettier violations in mobile changes

Apply curly: all rule to touch handlers — bare if (!touch) return and
if (!rect) return replaced with braced blocks. Run Prettier to fix
formatting in WaveformNavigator.tsx and mobile.spec.ts.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JMkf4XFMoJmBvVEJjErkXN

* Fix pre-existing Prettier formatting in markers test files

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JMkf4XFMoJmBvVEJjErkXN

* Add session-start hook to install dependencies on web sessions

Installs root and demo npm packages so that the linter (ESLint/Prettier),
unit tests (Vitest), and GitHub MCP server tools are all available from the
start of every remote session. Playwright OS-level deps are listed via
--dry-run since browser binary downloads are blocked by the environment proxy.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JMkf4XFMoJmBvVEJjErkXN

* Address PR review comments and fix CI lint failure

- Replace e.preventDefault() with CSS touch-action: none on .waveform-canvas;
  React registers touch listeners as passive in modern browsers so preventDefault
  was ineffective, touch-action reliably prevents scroll during waveform scrubbing
- Guard rect.width <= 0 in click and touch seek handlers to prevent NaN/Infinity
  seeks when the canvas is temporarily hidden or has no layout width
- Add onTouchCancel={onCanvasTouchEnd} so OS interruptions (incoming call, system
  gesture, multi-touch) clear the hover tooltip rather than leaving it stuck
- Feature-detect window.matchMedia before calling it in WaveformControls to guard
  against SSR and non-DOM environments where matchMedia may be undefined
- Add testIgnore: ['**/mobile.spec.ts'] to both desktop Playwright projects so the
  mobile spec only runs under mobile-chrome which has touch emulation enabled;
  prevents spurious failures and avoids triple-running the suite
- Replace Array.prototype.at(-1) with length-1 index in mobile test to fix the
  TypeScript TS2550 error (at() requires es2022 lib, tsconfig targets es2020)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JMkf4XFMoJmBvVEJjErkXN

* Fix integration tests: set window.__waveformReady in demo onPeaksComputed

All E2E specs (waveform.spec.ts, worker.spec.ts, mobile.spec.ts) wait for
window.__waveformReady === true before asserting. The flag was never set in
the demo app, causing every waitForFunction call to timeout (20 s each),
making worker.spec.ts tests fail outright (no .catch()) and the whole
integration job crawl to a halt.

Set the flag inside onPeaksComputed in the demo — the earliest reliable
signal that the waveform has fully rendered and is ready for interaction.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JMkf4XFMoJmBvVEJjErkXN

* Fix race condition: wait for audio metadata before tap seek test

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/KHeavyy/waveform-navigator/sessions/86cf2af4-3222-4b73-a67e-6fd3e085cb52

Co-authored-by: KHeavyy <7990261+KHeavyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor AudioContext error handling and update TypeScript configuration

* Refactor mobile integration tests for waveform readiness and improve tap interactions

* Add support for pre-computed peaks to enhance waveform rendering performance

- Introduced `precomputedPeaks` prop to `WaveformNavigator` for instant rendering.
- Updated `useWaveformData` to validate and utilize pre-computed peaks.
- Enhanced demo to showcase pre-computed peaks functionality.
- Added tests for pre-computed peaks handling in `useWaveformData`.

* Fix broken tests

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: KHeavyy <7990261+KHeavyy@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: fetchPhotos orderBy updatedAt (iOS uploads have no createdAt field)

iOS FirebasePhotoUploader only writes 'updatedAt', not 'createdAt'.
Firestore silently excludes documents missing the orderBy field,
causing all iOS photos to be invisible in the web app.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Migrate TV automation from Python/launchd to SmartThings Rules API

Replace the Mac-dependent Python scripts + launchd scheduler with
SmartThings cloud-based Rules that run independently:

- Morning rule: 6:30 AM Mon-Fri (turn on TV + launch browser)
- Evening rule: 5:00 PM Mon-Fri (turn on TV + launch browser)

Removed:
- scripts/launch_routine.py (Python TV launcher)
- scripts/morning-routine-tv.sh (shell wrapper)
- launchd plist (unloaded and deleted)
- .venv Python environment
- .gitignore entries for scripts/ artifacts

Updated SAMSUNG_TV_SETUP.md with SmartThings API commands, Rule IDs,
troubleshooting, and reproduce-from-scratch instructions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: MemoryManager + VmAllocator<T> memory layer (#20)

* refactor: replace Allocator/SimpleAllocator with MemoryManager + VmAllocator<T>

Implement the design from vm_allocator_architecture.md:

- MemoryManager (concrete, non-polymorphic) owns all Obj* and tracks
  bytes. create<T>() registers each object; collectAll() frees them.
- VmAllocBase provides rawAlloc/rawFree as a non-virtual base; MemoryManager
  inherits it so VmAllocator<T> only needs VmAllocBase* (no circular include).
- VmAllocator<T> is a minimal STL allocator routing to rawAlloc/rawFree.
  MyString = basic_string<char,...,VmAllocator<char>>.
- ObjString takes (string_view, VmAllocator<char>) — header tracked by
  create<ObjString>(), char buffer by VmAllocator<char>.
- Table uses composition (m_buckets: vector<Entry, VmAllocator<Entry>>)
  instead of private inheritance; explicit Table(VmAllocator<Entry>).
- Value stores Obj* instead of ObjHandle — mark-sweep GC never moves
  objects, so raw pointers are stable. Removes deref() indirection.
- Remove src/allocator.h, src/simple_allocator.h/cpp.
- debug, compiler, vm updated to use MemoryManager directly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: guard against null HOME env var in xdg_history_path

std::getenv("HOME") can return nullptr; passing nullptr to std::string()
is undefined behaviour, caught by clang-analyzer-cplusplus.StringChecker
(which WarningsAsErrors promotes to an error in our .clang-tidy config).
Also restore the rvalue makeString overload that was lost during the
refactor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: address review comments on PR #20

- vm_allocator.h: rename MyString → LoxString (naming consistency)
- object.h: MyString chars → LoxString chars (follows rename)
- memory_manager.h: trim verbose block comments
- value.cpp: clarify Obj* equality comment — interning is string-specific;
  other Obj types use identity equality
- test_allocator.cpp: rename PointerStability → HeapPointers_RemainValid...
  and add a comment explaining what the test verifies

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Tran Xuan Loc <tranxuanloc19920@gmail.com>

repository.name: SheetMusic_Viewer
repository.description: ScoreFlow 是一款專為專業樂手設計的高階樂譜閱讀器 (SheetMusic Viewer)，支援 macOS 與 iOS 平台。其核心設計目標是提供極致流暢的連續閱讀體驗，並具備專業級的圖層標記與場景跳轉功能。

commit.message:
refactor: simplify structure:list to always use last URL segment

Previously list mode used shortest-unique-suffix with os.Stat collision
avoidance (trying repo1 → pack/repo1 → org/pack/repo1). This was
complex and surprising when dirs already existed on disk.

New behavior:
- structure:list always returns the last /-delimited segment of
  repo.Name, stripping any trailing .git suffix
- Naming conflicts (two repos with the same last segment) are handled
  the same as any other skip: the second clone is skipped because its
  target directory already exists
- Use local_dir_name to resolve conflicts explicitly

Code changes:
- Replace ResolveDestName list-mode body with lastSegment() helper
- projPath parameter is no longer consulted (kept for API compat)
- Remove resolveDestNameTracked and allocation-tracking in
  ResolveDestNamesForProject; it now delegates to ResolveDestName
- dukh/server/monitor.go is unaffected (already uses
  ResolveDestNamesForProject from previous fix)

Tests: replace collision-avoidance tests with last-segment behaviour;
add .git stripping tests; add AlwaysLastSegmentEvenIfExists test

Docs: update README, STUDY, config.template.yaml, prd.md, architecture.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix flaky reminder integration tests with semaphore signaling (#573)

- Replace `Task.Delay` + counter polling with `SemaphoreSlim`
signaling
- Unregister reminders at the end of both `WithNewReminderName`
tests to prevent `ObjectDisposedException`
---------

commit.message:
fix: dukh now resolves repo paths correctly for list structure

- Export ExpandHome from internal/workspace (was unexported)
- Add ResolveDestNamesForProject: replicates init-time allocation order
  using an in-memory set instead of os.Stat, so dukh can find repos
  correctly even though all directories already exist when scanning
- Add resolveDestNameTracked private helper (same logic as
  ResolveDestName but allocation-set based rather than filesystem-based)
- Update monitor.scan() to use workspace.ExpandHome (fixes ~ in ws.Path)
  and workspace.ResolveDestNamesForProject (fixes list-structure paths)
- Simplify checkRepo signature: now receives pre-computed repoPath
- Add 6 new tests for ResolveDestNamesForProject covering tree mode,
  list no-collision, list collision, triple collision, localDirName
  override, and localDirName non-interference with suffix allocation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(viewer): add page number display via CSS without modifying DOM structure

- Added data-page attribute to .page-content-wrapper (non-breaking)
- Page number displayed via .page-content-wrapper::after pseudo-element
- Uses CSS attr(data-page) to avoid DOM mutations
- Only visible in horizontal mode
- Positioned at top-right corner (12px from edges)
- No JavaScript event handlers affected

Add .gitignore for clean repository

Clean up project structure and reorganize files

- Moved all images to images/ folder
- Archived old nested heck/ folder
- Created organized project structure
- Updated README with new file structure
- Removed duplicate files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(config): add workspace structure field (tree/list)

Adds a 'structure' field to workspace config controlling how repository
names containing '/' are mapped to local directories inside a project.

  tree (default): full name preserved as nested subdirs
    org/pack/repo → <project>/org/pack/repo

  list: shortest unique trailing suffix used as dest dir
    org/pack/repo → <project>/repo
    (falls back to pack/repo, then org/pack/repo if shorter paths exist)

Changes:
- internal/config: add StructureTree/StructureList constants;
  add Structure field to Workspace; RenderCloneCmd now takes a
  pre-computed destDir instead of projectPath (workspace layer owns
  the resolution logic).
- internal/workspace: add ResolveDestName(); update cloneRepo and
  pullRepo to use it; add 'strings' import.
- internal/config/config_test.go: update RenderCloneCmd callers to
  pass destDir directly.
- internal/workspace/workspace_test.go: add 7 new tests covering
  ResolveDestName and Init with list structure.
- config.template.yaml: add structure field + explanatory comment to
  both workspace entries.
- README.md, STUDY.md, docs/prd.md, docs/architecture.md: document
  the new field, modes, fallback logic, and ResolveDestName.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(shapes): expose rect-shape and circle-shape tools via pen toolbar

Previously, the rect-shape and circle-shape drawing tools existed in the Pens TOOLSET
but had no UI button to access them. This prevented users from using the shape tools.

Changes:
- Added 'pen-palette' (Pens) toolbar button to DockingBarManager
- Connected pen toolbar to EditSubBarManager infrastructure
- Pens toolbar now dynamically loads all Pens tools including rect/circle shapes
- Updated CSS state logic to track pen toolbar open/closed state
- Clarified potentialNudge state clearing in InteractionManager

Now users can:
1. Click the Pens button in the floating toolbar
2. Select rect-shape or circle-shape from the Pens toolbar
3. Draw rectangles and circles on the score with full drag support

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: Archipelago: a bridge puzzle game

commit.message:
fix(generate): install protoc-gen-go plugins inside just generate

The generate recipe now runs go install for both protoc-gen-go and
protoc-gen-go-grpc before invoking protoc, so the plugins are always
available regardless of the host machine state.

grazhda.sh simplified: plugin installation is now delegated to the
Justfile; the installer only ensures GOPATH/bin is on PATH before
calling just generate.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(shapes): clear potentialNudge state when starting new action to allow rect/circle drawing

The rect-shape and circle-shape tools were not responding to drag input because
leftover potentialNudge state from previous operations was intercepting drag
events in moveAction (lines 714-751). This caused new drawing attempts to enter
the 'nudge' branch instead of the 'drawing' branch.

Solution: Clear potentialNudge and isNudging at the start of proceedWithAction
(lines 515-516) to ensure each new action begins with clean state.

Fixes: shapes tools not accepting input after switching tools
Tested: rect-shape and circle-shape now respond to click-and-drag

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(annotation): restore 'self' source for backward compatibility with old stamps

When sources were synchronized from cloud storage, the 'self' source
was often removed from the sources list. However, old local stamps
retained sourceId: 'self', causing them to become invisible (filtered
out during rendering because their sourceId didn't match any source).

Fix: In loadStamps(), check if any stamps have sourceId: 'self' while
'self' is missing from sources. If so, restore the 'self' source to
the sources list before processing stamps.

This ensures all old annotations remain visible and editable.

Also removes debug logging from AnnotationRenderer and ViewerManager.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(installer): auto-install protoc-gen-go and protoc-gen-go-grpc

On a fresh machine protoc is available but the Go protobuf plugins
(protoc-gen-go, protoc-gen-go-grpc) are not, causing 'just generate'
to fail. Add install_protoc_plugins() which runs 'go install' for both
plugins when they are absent, and also ensures GOPATH/bin is on PATH
before protoc is invoked.

commit.message:
Update .github/workflows/ci.yml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add control-hosted DNS proxy

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: address remaining P1/P2/P3 review findings in Phase 3 explain design

P1 – Prompt injection: add sanitiseForPrompt() requirement; all client
string fields that enter LLM prompts must be whitelisted against declared
enum types; free-text fields prohibited by schema.

P1 – planRevision binding: document that planRevision must be a Cosmos
ETag or SHA-256 of the encrypted plan blob; add server-side regex
validation rule; update OptimizerExplainRequest doc comment.

P2 – Language: replace 'pseudonymised' with 'de-identified and minimised'
in Design constraints.

P2 – Remove provider from client consent struct: provider is now
server-selected and recorded in the audit record only; consent dialog
states provider *category* not exact name.

P2 – In-memory lifecycle: add step 5 specifying request-closure scoping,
no module-level persistence, GC reliance; add required unit test.

P2 – payloadHash in audit record: add payloadHash (server-computed
SHA-256 of canonical payload JSON) to retained audit fields; document
verifiability property.

P3 – ConsentScope typed enum: replace scope: string[] with
scope: ConsentScope[] backed by a new exported union type with six
named values and doc comments.

P3 – Retention and right to erasure: add 90-day TTL requirement, TTL
index mandate, and GDPR Article 17 deletion path requirement.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: remove yearRecords from explain payload; introduce OptimizationSummary

yearRecords contains per-year asset balances and income breakdowns that
are not needed for explanation generation. Introduce OptimizationSummary
as the minimised type sent to the server (aggregated fields + ruleProvenance
only). Explicitly document in §3.2 and §3.4 that yearRecords must not leave
the browser.

commit.message:
fix: two-pass hoist warmup and stdlib exemption

- Add WARMUP_PASS thread-local for two-pass compilation: warmup pass
  populates hoist module cache without caching the entry module's
  incomplete result; real pass uses fully populated cache
- Temporarily clear WARMUP_PASS inside resolve_for_hoist so hoist
  module compilations ARE cached during warmup
- Exempt stdlib modules from SUPPRESS_HOISTS (stdlib has no project
  dependencies and compiles cleanly in any order)
- Add invalidate_hir method to database for cache management

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: aivi
repository.description: A reactive, functional language for the Linux desktop

commit.message:
Add Experiment 7 API integration full-stack app

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: hoist resolution cascade — suppress nested project hoists, fix workspace root discovery

- Normalize empty workspace root ancestor to '.' (fixes fs::read_dir('') on Linux)
- Canonicalize paths in module_name_for_path (fixes strip_prefix for '.' root)
- Compile hoist modules with empty import stack (prevents false import-cycle errors)
- Add HOIST_COMPILING thread-local set for cycle detection across hoist compilations
- Add SUPPRESS_HOISTS flag to break recursive cascade: nested resolve_for_hoist
  returns cached results only for project modules, preventing incomplete results
  from being cached during the cascade
- Add opt-in AIVI_DEBUG_HOIST=1 logging for hoist resolution tracing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Improve room title restore and user-facing auth/upload errors

- Resolve room name on chat reload by hydrating from room list when store is empty
- Map login/register failures to friendly messages for common auth cases
- Harden upload API error handling for non-JSON failures and 413 payload-too-large
- Surface upload error messages in chat and profile toasts
- Update API client unit expectation for localized auth message

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: integrate dex-rugcheck-mcp and solana-rpc-mcp for pre-analysis workflow

Add support for two new local MCP servers (dex-rugcheck-mcp and
solana-rpc-mcp) to enable free token data gathering before paid svm402
analysis.

Changes:
- config.ts: Add DEX_RUGCHECK_MCP_PATH and SOLANA_RPC_MCP_PATH env vars
- index.ts: Spawn rugcheck and RPC clients matching existing patterns
- agent.ts: Register all new tools in READ_ONLY_TOOLS, knownTools, and
  system prompt capabilities; add TOKEN ANALYSIS WORKFLOW guidance
- configure.ts: Add new env vars to interactive menu
- docker-compose.yml: Comment out all local MCP paths as opt-in examples

commit.message:
Fix 7 reported bugs: private messaging, users list, uploads, auth errors, room password, room deletion

Bug 1: Users Online empty on join - Use serializeEvent() for initial
  history/users events in ChatController instead of manual serialization,
  ensuring proper type discrimination for polymorphic ChatMessage types.

Bug 2: Private messaging broken - Fix ChatService serialization to use
  objectMapper.valueToTree() for PrivateMessage and PrivateHistory events,
  matching the pattern used by other event types.

Bug 3/4: File/image upload + avatar upload - Backend compiles cleanly;
  NoClassDefFoundError was from running old build without clean compile.
  (Resolved by mvn clean compile)

Bug 5: Unfriendly auth error messages - Rework API client error handling
  to always attempt JSON parsing first, extract message/error fields from
  response body, fall back to generic 'Server error (status)' only when
  body is not JSON.

Bug 6: Wrong password allows private room entry - Add joinedRef tracking
  in useWebSocket; errors received before successful join (history/users)
  are treated as connection rejections. ChatPage navigates back to lobby
  on connection error.

Bug 7: Room owner can delete rooms - Add DELETE /api/rooms/{roomId}
  endpoint (owner-only), RoomRepository.delete() with cascade cleanup,
  RoomService.deleteRoom(), frontend API + store + UI with trash icon on
  room cards (visible on hover for room owner).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

CSS tweaks: resultsTable margin-inline 20px, remove main-container padding-bottom

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: suppress hoist-induced import-cycle false positives in stdlib

During workspace-hoist registration, stdlib modules are compiled
transitively with the app's import stack. When module_name_for_file
cannot resolve a logical name in that context it falls back to a
file-system path string (containing '/') as the module identifier.
This caused the cycle detector to report false positives like
  list -> math -> matrix -> list
and self-import cycles in nonEmpty.aivi and text.aivi.

Also fix foreign-span errors in hoisted modules by using the current
module's own FileId (self.module.file()) instead of hardcoded
FileId::new(0) for synthetic hoist spans.

Add resolve_for_hoist() to ImportResolver so hoist compilation can
signal cycles as Missing rather than Cycle, avoiding errors being
stored in the HIR of transitively-compiled stdlib modules.

Suppress any cycle where a path-encoded entry (containing '/') appears
in the cycle chain, as those entries only arise in hoist context and
never represent real application-code import cycles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: separate frontend and backend into dedicated directories

- Move Kotlin backend into backend/ subdirectory
  - Remove ServeStatic middleware and index.html route from Main.kt
  - Adjust DB and uploads paths to project root (parent of backend/)
  - Add maven-compiler-plugin config for JOOQ generated sources

- Create React frontend in frontend/
  - Vite + TypeScript + React + Zustand + Tailwind CSS
  - Preserve terminal aesthetic (IBM Plex Mono, green-on-black, ASCII)
  - Full TypeScript types matching all backend Kotlin models
  - API client with auth token management
  - WebSocket hook with reconnection and backoff
  - Zustand stores: auth, chat, room, ui
  - Pages: auth (login/register), lobby (rooms), chat
  - Features: messages, file/image upload, @mentions, reply-to,
    message edit/delete, search, private messages, user profiles
  - Vitest unit tests (24 tests for stores and API client)
  - Playwright E2E test scaffolds (auth, lobby, chat flows)
  - Vite proxy config for dev (API + WebSocket to backend :8000)

- Update README with new project structure and instructions

ci: add GitHub Actions publish workflow and project docs

.github/workflows/publish.yml
- Builds multi-platform image (linux/amd64 + linux/arm64) on push to main
  and on version tags (v*.*.*)
- Pushes to both ghcr.io/i84762/copilot-agent-docker and Docker Hub
- Uses docker/metadata-action for automatic semver + SHA tagging
- Layer cache via ghcr.io buildcache to speed up CI builds
- Updates Docker Hub README from README.md on each main push
- Build-only (no push) on pull_request for Dockerfile validation
- Supports workflow_dispatch for manual image rebuilds

.env.example — documents all supported environment variables:
  GH_TOKEN, PROJECT_PATH, COPILOT_TASK / COPILOT_TASK_FILE,
  COPILOT_INSTRUCTIONS_REPO/FILE/BRANCH, GIT_USER_NAME/EMAIL,
  FLUTTER_VERSION, GO_VERSION, FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID,
  GCLOUD_KEY_FILE, GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS,
  GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_JSON, FIREBASE_TEST_DEVICE, FIREBASE_TEST_TIMEOUT

README.md — full usage documentation:
  Quick start, task resolution priority table, Firebase setup guide,
  supported project types table, SDK cache notes, file structure

repository.name: copilot-agent-docker
repository.description: Run GitHub Copilot CLI autonomously in Docker on any local project — auto-detects Flutter/Java/Node/Python/Go/Rust, Firebase Test Lab, change reports

commit.message:
docs: add sample global Copilot instructions template

sample-instructions/copilot-instructions.md — host this in your own
GitHub repo and reference it via -InstructionsRepo:

- Identity: autonomous agent, no confirmation prompts
- Safety rules: repo deletion forbidden, secrets never logged
- Commit rules: frequent commits, conventional format, Co-authored-by trailer
- Error handling: diagnose and fix automatically, never give up after one attempt
- Code style: follow existing conventions, grep before editing
- Testing: flutter test first, then ftl-test for device tests
- Reporting: mandatory generate-report call at task completion,
  tee test/Firebase output to /tmp for inclusion in report
- Output format: concise explanations, TASK COMPLETE summary on finish

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
